title: carex salina subsp. cuspidata
intro: Carex salina subsp. cuspidata, also known as Salt-spray sedge, is a subspecies of sedge plant that belongs to the genus Carex and is native to coastal regions of North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its rich content of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Carex salina subsp. cuspidata include its potential to treat various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Carex salina subsp. cuspidata can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
